fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Chromium-51?

{{c1::Nuclear reactor (neutron activation)}}
fields.Extra:
Chromium-51
Production: Nuclear reactor (neutron activation)
Decay: Electron capture (100%)
Principal photon energies: 320 keV
Half-life: 27.8 days

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Fluorine-18?

{{c1::Cyclotron}}
fields.Extra:
Fluorine-18
Production: Cyclotron
Decay: Beta⁺ (97%), EC (3%)
Principal photon energies: 511 keV (annihilation)
Half-life: 110 minutes
Notes: Used in FDG PET. Trapped via phosphorylation (hexokinase). NaF critical organ: Bladder. Brown fat uptake (neck/shoulders). Insulin causes muscle uptake. HCC/Mucinous/RCC often cold. Major spill limit: 100 mCi.


Additional images

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Gallium-67?

{{c1::Cyclotron}}
fields.Extra:
Gallium-67
Production: Cyclotron
Decay: Electron capture (100%)
Principal photon energies: 93, 184, 300, 393 keV
Half-life: 78 hours
Notes: Binds transferrin/lactoferrin. Critical organ: Colon. Sarcoid signs: Panda (lacrimal/parotid), Lambda (hilar/paratracheal). Hot in Lymphoma (vs. Cold Toxo). Major spill limit: 10 mCi.


Additional images

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Gallium-68?

{{c1::Eluted from a Ge-68/Ga-68 generator}}
fields.Extra:
Gallium-68
Production: Eluted from a Ge-68/Ga-68 generator
Decay: Positron emission (β+, ~89%) and electron capture (~11%) to stable Zn-68
Principal photon energies: 511 keV (annihilation photons)
Half-life: 68 minutes
Notes: Major spill limit: 20 mCi.


Additional images

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Indium-111?

{{c1::Cyclotron}}
fields.Extra:
Indium-111
Production: Cyclotron
Decay: Electron capture (100%)
Principal photon energies: 171, 245 keV
Half-life: 67 hours
Notes: Used in tagged white blood cell scans, somatostatin receptor imaging of neuroendocrine tumors (e.g., carcinoid, gastrinoma), and radionuclide cisternography/CSF studies. WBC/Octreotide critical organ: Spleen. ProstaScint critical organ: Liver. Octreotide binds somatostatin receptors (2 & 5). Major spill limit: 10 mCi.


Additional images

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Iodine-123?

{{c1::Cyclotron}}
fields.Extra:
Iodine-123
Production: Cyclotron
Decay: Electron capture (100%)
Principal photon energies: 159 keV
Half-life: 13.2 hours
Notes: Used in Ioflupane-123, a cocaine analog, for DAT scans. Organified. MIBG adrenals seen 75% (vs 15% with I-131). Breastfeeding: Resume 2-3 days. Does not localize in the osseous skeleton as part of normal physiologic distribution; physiologic distribution is in the nasopharynx, salivary glands, thyroid, stomach, bladder, and bowel. Major spill limit: 10 mCi.


Additional images

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Iodine-131?

{{c1::Nuclear reactor (U-235 fission)}}
fields.Extra:
Iodine-131
Production: Nuclear reactor (U-235 fission)
Decay: Beta⁻ (100%)
Principal photon energies: 284, 364, 637 keV
Half-life: 8 days
Notes: Organified. Contraindicated in kids/pregnancy. Breastfeeding: Stop completely (pump & dump). Does not localize in the osseous skeleton as part of normal physiologic distribution; physiologic distribution is in the nasopharynx, salivary glands, thyroid, stomach, bladder, and bowel. Major spill limit: 1 mCi.


Additional images

fields.Text:
What is the mode of decay of Iodine-131?

{{c1::Beta⁻ (100%)}}

Decay: Beta⁻ (100%)
Principal photon energies: 113, 208 keV
Half-life: 6.65 days
Notes: Used in targeted radionuclide therapy (e.g., DOTATATE, PSMA). 208 = New SaFe. Major spill limit: 2 mCi.

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Samarium-153?

{{c1::Nuclear reactor (U-235 fission)}}
fields.Extra:
Samarium-153
Production: Nuclear reactor (U-235 fission)
Decay: Beta⁻ (100%)
Principal photon energies: 103 keV
Half-life: 46 hours
Notes: Complexes w/ hydroxyapatite. Beta & Gamma (103 keV) emitter. Renal excretion. Moderate marrow toxicity (recovery 6-8wks).

What is the method of production of Technetium-99m?

{{c1::Generator product}}
fields.Extra:
Technetium-99m
Production: Generator product
Decay: Isomeric transition (100%)
Principal photon energies: 140 keV
Half-life: 6.02 hours
Notes: Trapped but not organified. Breastfeeding: Resume 12-24h. Sestamibi: Mitochondria/passive diffusion. Major spill limit: 100 mCi.

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Thallium-201?

{{c1::Cyclotron}}
fields.Extra:
Thallium-201
Production: Cyclotron
Decay: Electron capture (100%)
Principal photon energies: 69–80, 167 keV
Half-life: 73.1 hours
Notes: Potassium analog. Na/K pump (active). Redistributes (viability). Lymphoma Hot (vs Toxo Cold). Critical organ: Renal Cortex. Major spill limit: 100 mCi.

Principal photon energies: 81 keV
Half-life: 5.3 days
Notes: Fat soluble (RUQ uptake = fatty liver). Retained in COPD (air trapping) during washout.

Nitrogen-13

Decay: Beta⁺ (100%)
Principal photon energies: 511 keV (annihilation)
Half-life: 10 minutes

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Rubidium-82?

{{c1::Generator product}}
fields.Extra:
Rubidium-82
Production: Generator product
Decay: Beta⁺ (100%)
Principal photon energies: 511 keV (annihilation)
Half-life: 75 seconds
Notes: Used for heart imaging. Potassium analog (Na/K pump). Major spill limit: 10 mCi.

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Radium-223?

{{c1::Nuclear reactor / Generator product}}
fields.Extra:
Radium-223
Production: Nuclear reactor / Generator product
Decay: Alpha (100%)
Principal photon energies: Multiple low-energy gammas
Half-life: 11 days
Notes: Calcium analog. Emits 4 Alpha particles. GI excretion. Least marrow toxicity. Survival benefit (Prostate).

fields.Text:
What is the method of production of Yttrium-90?

{{c1::Nuclear reactor (from Sr-90 decay)}}
fields.Extra:
Yttrium-90
Production: Nuclear reactor (from Sr-90 decay)
Decay: Beta⁻ (100%)
Principal photon energies: Negligible gamma; Bremsstrahlung only
Half-life: 64 hours
Notes: Pure Beta. 10mm tissue penetration. Pre-tx lung shunt (MAA) must be <20%. Used in Zevalin (CD20). Major spill limit: 1 mCi.
